title: rosa caesia subsp. subcollina
intro: Rosa caesia subsp. subcollina, also known as the Himalayan wild rose, is a species of rose that is native to the Himalayas and is characterized by its fragrant white flowers and dark green foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rosa caesia subsp. subcollina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. Rosa caesia subsp. subcollina is typically prepared in herbal tea form or as a tincture, and can also be used in topical applications such as creams and salves.
